content.problemStatement:
After winning the Candidates Tournament, Suzumo is intensively training for his upcoming match against Magus Cartesian.

In order to improve his calculation skills, Suzumo invented a new game, which he named PawnChess. PawnChess is a two-player game played on a row of NNN cells. Its rules are as follows:


Initially, some pawns are placed in some cells; each pawn is either black or white. There may be at most one pawn in each cell at any time. For each white pawn, all the pawns that are to the left of this pawn are also white.
Let's call the players White and Black. Each player may only move pawns of their colour. The players alternate turns; White moves first.
In White's turn, White must move one pawn one cell to the right (if the pawn was in cell iii, it is moved to cell i+1i+1i+1). Similarly, in Black's turn, Black must move one pawn one cell to the left (from cell iii to cell i−1i-1i−1). It is not allowed to move a pawn outside of the row of cells or to move a pawn to a cell which already contains a pawn with the same colour.
If a pawn PPP is moved to an empty cell, nothing happens. If it is moved to a cell containing a pawn RRR with the opposite colour, the pawn RRR is captured — removed from the game, and PPP is placed in the cell which previously contained RRR.
The winner of the game is the player that captures all the pieces of their opponent. Note that as long as there is at least one black and at least one white pawn, each player can make a move.
Given the initial configuration of the game board, help Suzumo determine the winner of the game. Assume that both players play optimally.
constraints: There is at least one white pawn and at least one black pawn
All pawns to the left of a white pawn are always white
The sum of N over all test cases is at most 5 * 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first and only line of each test case contains a single string S with length N describing the game board. Each character in S is '.', 'W' or 'B' denoting an empty cell, a white pawn and a black pawn respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string 'W' if White wins the game or 'B' if Black wins.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, White wins because there is a white pawn before the black pawn. In the second test case, Black wins because there is a black pawn before the white pawn.

solution.approach: To determine the winner of the game, we can analyze the initial configuration of the game board. We can iterate through the string from left to right and keep track of the number of white pawns and black pawns. If we encounter a black pawn before a white pawn, we can immediately conclude that Black wins. Otherwise, we can conclude that White wins.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the length of the string
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the input and output
